title: rubus anisodontus
intro: Rubus anisodontus, also known as the Fijian raspberry or wild raspberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family, native to the Pacific islands, particularly Fiji. The plant's leaves and stems have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rubus anisodontus include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its ability to treat digestive issues and reduce fever. Rubus anisodontus is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and its leaves and stems can also be used fresh in salads or as a garnish.
